# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent edge computing security.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { VercelRequest, VercelResponse } from '@vercel/node';

export default async function handler(req: VercelRequest, res: VercelResponse) {
  if (req.method !== 'POST') {
    res.status(405).send('Method Not Allowed');
    return;
  }

  try {
    const { content, headers } = req.body;

    // Validate content and headers
    if (typeof content !== 'string' || !Array.isArray(headers)) {
      res.status(400).send('Invalid input');
      return;
    }

    const allowedHeaders = ['Content-Type', 'X-Custom-Header'];
    const responseHeaders: Record<string, string> = {};

    headers.forEach(header => {
      if (allowedHeaders.includes(header.name) && typeof header.value === 'string') {
        responseHeaders[header.name] = header.value;
      }
    });

    const stream = new ReadableStream({
      start(controller) {
        controller.enqueue(content);
        controller.close();
      }
    });

    const response = new Response(stream, {
      headers: responseHeaders
    });

    response.body.pipeTo(res);
  } catch (error) {
    res.status(500).send('Internal Server Error');
  }
}
